key_points: Atom is the smallest particle of an element that can take part in a chemical reaction
Molecule is the smallest particle of a substance that can exist independently and retain its chemical properties
Elements are made of only one type of atom
Compounds are made of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions
Chemical formulas represent the types and numbers of atoms in a molecule (e.g., H₂O, CO₂)
Law of constant proportion: a chemical compound always contains the same elements in the same proportion by mass
examples: Water (H₂O) is a compound of hydrogen and oxygen
Oxygen (O₂) and Nitrogen (N₂) are molecules of elements
Salt (NaCl) is a compound with 1 sodium and 1 chlorine atom per molecule
formulas: Molecular mass = Sum of atomic masses of all atoms in the molecule
Mass percent of an element in compound = (Mass of element / Mass of compound) × 100
exam_tips: Distinguish between atom and molecule with examples
Remember formulas of common molecules and compounds
Use law of constant proportion to solve problems
Calculate molecular mass and percentage composition of elements in compounds
Give examples of elements and compounds clearly in exams
